title: GitOps & Azure Dev/Test offer
description: Use GitOps in association with Azure Dev/Test
summary: GitOps is an operational framework. It takes DevOps best practices used for developing applications and applies them to infrastructure automation. When teams practice GitOps, they use configuration files stored as code (infrastructure as code). These files generate the same environment every time it's deployed. Think of it like application source code generating the same application binaries every time you build.
classification_reason: Conceptual explanation of GitOps applied to Azure Dev/Test; no product-specific configuration values, thresholds, or decision matrices.

title: Add users to your Azure Dev/Test developer directory tenant
description: A how-to guide for adding users to your Azure credit subscription and managing their access with role-based controls.
summary: To manage and access subscription resources, you must be a user within the directory. In the subscriptions themselves, the hierarchy is controlled by the Microsoft Entra tenancies. Microsoft Entra ID is a lightweight directory protocol (LDAP) that stores account names and passwords. Before adding users, you need to determine your business hierarchy and what level of access they need within your subscription.
classification_reason: Covers adding users and managing access conceptually; summary does not show concrete RBAC role names, permission scopes, or detailed security configuration tables.

title: Removing credit limits and changing Azure Dev/Test offers
description: How to remove credit limits and change Azure Dev/Test offers. Switch from pay-as-you-go to another offer.
summary: If you currently have a subscription with pay-as-you-go rates, you can switch your Azure subscription to another offer. Your reasons for changing offers might be specific to your organizational structure. But here are some common reasons you might want to change your subscription offer or remove credit limits. Changing your subscription offer is easy for certain offer types. You can followour Switching Azure offers documentation, or contact support for more assistance.

title: What is Azure Dev/Test offer?
description: Use the Azure Dev/Test offer to get Azure credits for Visual Studio subscribers.
summary: This article explains the Azure Dev/Test offer that comes with your Visual Studio subscription benefits. With this offer, you get some Azure monthly credits to explore and try various Azure services at no cost.  Credit amounts vary by subscription level.  Users can also benefit from discounted Azure dev/test rates for various Azure services, enabling cost-efficient development and testing.
classification_reason: High-level overview of Azure Dev/Test offer and credits; no specific numeric limits, configuration parameters, or detailed decision/troubleshooting content.

description: As a Visual Studio subscriber, learn how to access an Azure Credit subscription.

Note The email address you use for sign in dictates what Microsoft Entra tenant your subscription is associated with.
classification_reason: Quickstart on accessing Azure credit subscription; mainly sign-in choices and tenant association, without detailed configuration tables, limits, or troubleshooting mappings.

title: Troubleshoot expired Visual Studio subscription
display_title: Troubleshoot and recover expired Visual Studio subscriptions
description: Learn how to renew an expired subscription, purchase a new one, or transfer your Azure resources.
summary: If your Visual Studio subscription expires or is removed, all the subscription benefits, including the monthly Azure dev/test individual credit are no longer available. To continue using Azure with a monthly credit, you need to renew your subscription, purchase a new subscription, and/or transfer your Azure resources to a different Azure subscription that includes the Azure dev/test individual credit. Important You must transfer your resources to another Azure subscription before your current Az
classification_reason: Focused on the specific symptom of an expired or removed Visual Studio subscription and how to resolve it (renew, purchase new, or transfer resources). Contains product-specific guidance and constraints (e.g., transfer resources before subscription ends), fitting a troubleshooting symptom→solution pattern.
